rulings.Case Title: CALDERA PHARMECEUTICALS, INC., A DELAWARE VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 10, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANTS THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, and, LOS ALAMOS NATIONAL SECURITY, L.L.C. Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings. Is granted, without leave to amend. Plaintiff's complaint and theory of recovery are based upon substantial rules of patent law. No reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: GRANT & WEBER, A CORPORATION VS. MAX FRUMES et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 10, 2010, line 3, DEFENDANT MAX FRUMES MOTION TO SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT. Is denied. Defendant failed to provide copy of proposed responsive pleading with motion, as required by Code of Civil Procedure section 473(b). =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: VICTOR BOLANO VS. BURLINGTON COAT FACTORY, BUSINESS FORM UNKNOWN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Relief Per Ccp 473, Or In The Alternative, Motion For Judgment On The Pleding Per Ccp 438
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 10, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANTS BURLINGTON COAT FACTORY OF CALIFORNIA, LLC, and, SALVADOR MARROQUIN Motion for Relief Per CCP 473, or in the Alternative, Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings Per CCP 438. Motion denied. The complaint was filed on 5/18/2009. An amendment to the complaint was filed 6/11/2009 substituting the proper name for a Doe. The first amended complaint was properly filed on 7/6/2009 before defendant answered. Defendant filed its answer to the first amended complaint on 9/17/2009. Defendant's failure to read the first amended complaint does not constitute mistake, inadvertence, surprise or excusable neglect warranting relief pursuant to CCP section 473. No good cause shown to continue the MSJ hearing date or shorten the notice period. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JP BUILDERS INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. LISA LEEBOVE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Special Motion To Strike(Anti-Slapp) First, Second, And Third Claims For Relief Of X-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 10, 2010, line 7, CROSS DEFENDANT JP BUILDERS INC. Special Motion to Strike (Anti-Slapp) First, Second, and Third Claims for Relief of Cross-Complaint. The anti-SLAPP motion was meritorious and cross-defendant is the prevailing party. Defendant is awarded $7,429 as reasonable attorney fees and costs in the prosecution of its anti-SLAPP Motion. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: REBECCA CAHOON et al VS. CARLSON BOULEVARD LP DBA PULLMAN POINT et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 10, 2010, line 9, DEFENDANT CARLSON BOULEVARD LP DBA PULLMAN POINT MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Is granted with 10 days leave to amend. Plaintiff must allege despicable and willful conduct. Plaintiffs have not alleged any intentional torts, or that Pullman was specially aware of the roofers' conduct, or how Pullman is liable for the roofers' acts. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JEREMY GROSS et al VS. GRANT GRIFFITHS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration Of All Claims Brought By Pltfs And Dismiss Or Stay The Action, Or In The Alternative, To Stay Action Pending Arbitration;
